Title: How to embrace AI in coding: Tabnine, Github Copilot, Cursor, Augment Code | Arsène Touck posted on the topic | LinkedIn
Text:
Social networks, these days for a developer are like an “Escherian stairwell”, whether you go up or down, you seem to come back to the same place leading to a publication about AI whether it's vibe coding, agents, LLMs or many others. So just like the “Escherian stairwell”, it's better to understand how amazing it is and embrace it than to run away from it in fear. Start experimenting at your own pace : Tabnine, Github Copilot, Cursor, Augment Code, etc. Adopt the one that best suits your case and enjoy. #Resilience #VibeCoding #Dev #Attitude #Grit

New AI tools pop up weekly: Copilot, Cursor, n8n, MCP servers. Learning one? Easy. Watch demos, skim docs, experiment. But integrating them into a cohesive developer workflow? That's the challenge. 🔹 Do they actually help you ship software? 🔹 Can they be trusted in CI/CD pipelines? 🔹 Where do coding assistants end, and agents begin? AI Dev Tools Zoomcamp answers these questions. You'll use AI tools to build complete projects: 🔹 Ship a React + FastAPI app with AI assistance 🔹 Connect assistants to GitHub and databases with MCP 🔹 Build your own coding agent for Django projects 🔹 Automate DevOps tasks (testing, PR reviews, incident response) 🔹 Create no-code AI workflows with n8n First cohort starts November 18, 2025 Register here: https://lnkd.in/e8bpVbtJ

✨ Agent Engineering Bootcamp, Week 3, Day 3 with Meri Nova and Hai N. on Maven. 🌅 Morning Live Coding Session Today’s session brought the agentic stack to life with real-time MCP integration and discovery. Hai showed how to wire up a Firecrawl MCP server, initialize the client, and connect it through service + events. The agent discovered and used six tools, including Firecrawl Scrape and Search, enabling live web scraping and search without building tools manually. 📌 Some highlights: • Scraped a Wikipedia page and extracted structured Markdown and HTML • Logged real-time tool discovery and usage for better observability • Used scrape and search tools to retrieve site data • Community builders shared their MCP experiments ☀️ Afternoon Lecture Meri Nova led a thoughtful discussion, followed by Hai on LLM evals as the backbone of reliable AI systems. Guest builder Delaney Thompson from Caddie AI shared her founder journey. 🛠️ Key insights: • Evals bring visibility and accountability to model behavior • Tracing and evals act like unit tests, catching regressions and drift • Domain-specific evals align models to product priorities • Guardrails run alongside model calls for real-time checks • Tools like Langsmith and Braintrust make evaluation easy 🚀 Delaney shared her founder's journey: started with a simple landing page and cold emails, built iteratively, and centered everything on solving real problems with clarity and heart. She turned pain points into a thriving AI business without a tech background. 💡 Key Takeaway MCP servers, live scraping, and eval pipelines are turning agent engineering into a real discipline where observability, adaptability, and creativity meet production challenges. #AgentEngineering #AI #ContextEngineering #LearningInPublic #FutureOfWork

🔥 The next big thing is here — 𝐎𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐥𝐨𝐰 𝐀𝐈 from Stack Overflow! As the coding world keeps evolving with AI-powered tools, Stack Overflow just made a powerful move to stay ahead. 🚀 𝐎𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐟𝐥𝐨𝐰 𝐀𝐈 is not just another coding assistant — it’s built on 𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐟𝐢𝐞𝐝 𝐝𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐞𝐫 𝐤𝐧𝐨𝐰𝐥𝐞𝐝𝐠𝐞 from the Stack Overflow community itself. That means every suggestion, fix, or explanation comes from real-world, battle-tested developer experience. 💡 This tool can help with: ⚙️ Debugging & code optimization 💬 Explaining complex errors 🧠 Learning from trusted industry data Honestly, this could be a game changer for both 𝐧𝐞𝐰 𝐝𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐨𝐩𝐞𝐫𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐬𝐞𝐚𝐬𝐨𝐧𝐞𝐝 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐟𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐥𝐬 who want reliable, explainable AI help — not just code generation. If you haven’t tried it yet, it’s worth exploring. The link’s in the comments 👇 #StackOverflow #OverflowAI #ArtificialIntelligence #Coding #Developers #Programming #TechInnovation #AIRevolution #MaheshPerera #ProductivityExpert #FutureOfCode

AI-assisted coding isn’t a side experiment anymore — it’s becoming infrastructure. Developers using tools like GitHub Copilot report completing tasks 55% faster, and adoption is set to cross 84% by 2025. But speed is only part of the story. From onboarding junior engineers to rebalancing workflows around design and problem-solving, AI is quietly reshaping how software gets built. In our latest blog, we break down: - Why developers keep reaching for AI - The tools worth knowing now - Where AI fits (and doesn’t) in workflows - How tech leaders can balance speed with fundamentals Learn more: https://hubs.la/Q03PQQ0r0 #AIAssistedCoding #GitHubCopilot

The Future of Coding is a Vibe ☕ Imagine this — you’re sitting with your coffee… and your app is getting built in the background. No endless typing. No debugging nightmares. Just you… your idea… and AI doing the rest. That’s what Vibe Coding is all about. A world where we don’t just write code — we create it with our vibe. AI tools like Cursor, Copilot, Gemini, and Windsurf are already making this real. You tell them what you want, they understand your intent, and start building with you — line by line, logic by logic. It’s not about replacing developers. It’s about freeing us from repetitive work so we can focus on what really matters — ideas, creativity, and building experiences that feel human. 🎥 I shared my thoughts on this in my latest video: 👉 https://lnkd.in/dnJB3Gst And trust me, once you understand this concept… you’ll never look at coding the same way again. Are you ready for the Vibe Coding era? #VibeCoding #AItools #FutureOfCoding #TheCodeParadox #OpenAI #CursorAI #GitHubCopilot #Gemini #AIDevelopment #TechRevolution

🚀 AI + Developer = The Future of Coding As a Software Engineer, there was a time when we spent hours debugging, rewriting, and refactoring code. Now, with tools like GitHub Copilot, we’re not just coding faster — we’re thinking smarter. Copilot doesn’t replace creativity — it amplifies it. It helps you focus on what really matters: building logic, solving problems, and turning ideas into innovation. When AI writes the routine code, You write the future. Embrace AI-assisted coding — not as a shortcut, but as a superpower that enhances your craft. Because the next generation of developers won’t just write code — they’ll design intelligence. Let’s build the future, one intelligent line at a time 💻 #GitHubCopilot #SoftwareEngineering #AIForDevelopers #FutureOfWork #Innovation #DeveloperCommunity #ArtificialIntelligence

🤖 How I’m Using AI Tools to Code Smarter (Not Harder) AI isn’t replacing developers, it’s upgrading us. Over the past few months, I’ve been experimenting with tools like GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Codeium, and here’s what actually stuck: 💡 1. Idea exploration with Copilot When I’m designing APIs or refactoring services in Spring Boot, I use Copilot not for answers but for options. It helps me see multiple approaches before picking the best one. ⚙️ 2. Speeding up boilerplate with Cursor Generating DTOs, test data, or config templates is no longer a drag. Cursor’s inline chat saves me 20–30 minutes daily. 🧠 3. Code reviews made sharper Before pushing, I run my diffs through an AI assistant to catch missing validations, null checks, and performance issues. It’s like an extra pair of senior-dev eyes. The goal isn’t to let AI write your code, it’s to free your mind for the hard parts: logic, architecture, and creativity. Curious to know what one AI tool has actually made your dev life easier? #AI #DeveloperTools #Productivity #GitHubCopilot #Codeium #Cursor #SoftwareEngineering #SpringBoot

𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝘁𝗲𝗮𝗺 “𝗗𝗼𝗰𝘀 & 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗰𝗸 𝗢𝘃𝗲𝗿𝗳𝗹𝗼𝘄” 𝗼𝗿 𝘁𝗲𝗮𝗺 “𝗔𝗜”? For years, the survival kit was clear: 📖 Documentation for the official truth. 💡 Stack Overflow for real-world fixes and community wisdom. Together, they’ve been the backbone of developer problem-solving. Reliable, battle-tested — but sometimes slow, scattered, and (let’s admit it) a bit painful to navigate. 𝙉𝙤𝙬 𝘼𝙄 𝙝𝙖𝙨 𝙠𝙞𝙘𝙠𝙚𝙙 𝙞𝙣 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙙𝙤𝙤𝙧. 🚀 It’s instant. It’s interactive. It feels like having a coding buddy who never sleeps. You drop in your messy code and error logs, and boom — tailored answers in seconds. But speed comes with a catch: no peer review, no community debates, and sometimes… confidently wrong solutions. But here what i think the truth is: Docs + Stack Overflow are the library of knowledge. AI is the study partner. The real power isn’t choosing one over the other — it’s knowing when to lean on each. Use AI to move fast, then double-check with the docs and community before you ship. Will save you alot. 💬 So, what’s your workflow right now — are you reaching first for AI, or do you still trust the good old docs + Stack Overflow combo? #ContinuousLearning #Productivity #Developers #Coding #Programming #SoftwareEngineering #DevTools

It’s funny how a single conversation can make you realize how much the tech landscape has changed. Today, a colleague and I were reminiscing about the pre-AI era of coding. We were trying to remember how long it used to take to build and debug a single application. 🤔 The process was something like: 1. Hit a cryptic error. 2. Scour Stack Overflow for 45 minutes. 3. Find a thread with a similar but not identical issue. 4. Try 12 different solutions from the comments. 5. Finally get it working, but not entirely sure why. We had a good laugh, but it was a stark reminder of the sheer grind of software development back then. The debugging marathons, the endless tabs of documentation, and the triumphant feeling of finally fixing a bug after hours of work. The power of AI-assisted development with tools like GitHub Copilot and ChatGPT is truly revolutionary. It’s not just about speed; it's about reducing cognitive load and letting us focus on architecture and complex problem-solving. #ThrowbackTech: What’s your most memorable "pre-AI" debugging war story? How long did that one stubborn bug haunt you? #SoftwareDevelopment #AI #DeveloperTools #Coding #Debugging #TechEvolution #Programming #StackOverflow #CSharp #DotNet #DotNetCore
